title: podman run container id to file
body:
podman run --cidfile /tmp/foo writes the container's id
to a file.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think we either rollback or attempt to create the CIDFILE in the beginning, and remove it on error.
I don't think deleting the (potentially now running) container is a good option, though we should definitely return nonzero if the CID file was not created
well you cannot get the container ID, unless we change the wiring, until we create the container.  So just need to decide, rest is just implementation.
Should be fine to return nonzero but leave the container. Might want to do that before we actually start the container in run, though?

How about you do 
```
 +		if err := libpod.WriteFile("", c.String("cidfile")); err != nil { 
```
before creating the container, and then return error on failure.  This leaves a small chance that the second file will fail, but much less likely, then return the second error.

:pushpin: Commit 6194b10 has been approved by `rhatdan`
